rulings.Case Title: RONALD SMITH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 1. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION denied. Triable issue of fact raised re: exposure and causation. (Declarations of plaintiff and Ay.) Failure to shift burden re: summary adjudication of false representation and premises owner/contractor liability causes of action. =(302/CWW/VC)
Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 2. DEFENDANT PLANT INSULATION COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to March 13, 2009 per stipulation of the parties. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JESS TORRES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To Tax Costs Of Defendant Plant Insulation Company
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 3. PLAINTIFF JESS TORRES' Motion To Tax Costs Of Defendant Plant Insulation Company is off calendar at the request of the moving party. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MARCELLA FAULK VS. ANTHONY BASS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Or For An Order Setting Matter For Trial
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 4. PLAINTIFF MARCELLA FAULK'S Motion For Enforcement Of Settlement Or For An Order Setting Matter For Trial is denied. Court cannot enter judgment in favor of plaintiff because there is insufficient showing of defendant's direct participation in the stipulation for entry of judgment. See Levy v. Superior Court (1995) 10 Cal 4th 578. Plaintiff to contact Dept. 212 to be placed back on the trial calendar. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT VANDERLEI PEREIRA'S Motion To Strike Plaintiff Progressive's Doe Amendment is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DOLORES STREET HOLDINGS, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTERED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 9. RESPONDENT BOARD OF APPEALS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/PROHIBITION/CERTIFICATION. OBJECTIONS TO REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE OVERRULED. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. =(302/CWW/BD)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES P QUINN VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, March 9, 2009, line 10. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION BOARD'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Demurrer sustained without leave to amend. Failure to name tenants as parties to this action. 90 day statute of limitations applies. See Liang v. S.F. Residential Rent Stabilization and Arbitration Board 124 C.A. 4th 775, 778-779; CCP section 1094.6(b). =(302/CWW/VC)
